In this performance, audiences will experience the story of how the turtle carried our world on her back, and how the tiny muskrat succeeded in giving us the greatest gift of all - a place to call home, Turtle Island. Chad Solomon is a member of the Henvey Inlet, First Nation. As a child, he would visit with his late grandparents. Chad's gran

events.title: The Magic Mel Show
events.description: Find your "inner magic" with The Amazing Magic Mel during this upbeat, hilarious, and interactive magical experience. Expect a healthy dose of participation and self-empowerment as Mel leads aspiring magicians of all ages on a journey to become "official magicians" during this fun, fast-paced magic show for the entire family.  For children of all ages with their parents or caregivers. Drop-in, no registration required.

(2015) 91 min. Rated G August 21 - Minions: The Rise of Gru. (2022) 88 min. Rated G Drop-in. No registration needed.

events.title: Unconventional Materials: An Osborne Collection Exhibit
events.description: An exhibition of intricately crafted books, artworks and much more. The Unconventional Materials exhibit highlights the use of unconventional techniques and materials in children’s literature, featuring historical and modern examples.   Visit the Osborne Collection to experience our exhibit featuring items such as plasticine art by Barbara Reid, dioramas and panoramas, postcards and other ephemera, and hornbooks and battledores. Located on the 4th floor of the Lillian H. Smith Branch. Free.

events.title: Author Visit: Jane and the Blue Willow Princess with Catherine Little
events.description: “On a beautiful spring day, sisters Jane and Cassandra enjoy tea with Mother in the garden. Jane is in the throes of writing a story for Father’s birthday, but too many ideas swirl in her head, and she cannot get started. The Blue Willow pattern on the tea set catches Jane’s attention … will that be the inspiration she’s looking for?​” Catherine Little presents a reading from her picture book "Jane and the Blue Willow Princess" about author Jane Austen, then leads a fun craft with air-dry clay.

events.title: Diving Deep: Wax Resist Underwater World Art
events.description: Learn to draw and paint your very own underwater scene using pastels and watercolours! Explore the magical world beneath the sea as you add vibrant colours to your artwork, and discover how to create stunning textures by sprinkling salt on watercolours. This fun, hands-on class will inspire imagination while teaching techniques that bring the underwater world to life with depth and texture. events.title: The Hidden Rivers
events.description: Participants will learn about the hidden rivers in Toronto and explore how our changing cityscape impacts on our waterways and ecosystems. Looking at archival images and current references, participants will be guided to bring their own stories into our city’s images using material such as watercolours, crayons, playdough, text, etc onto colouring pages. Led by Shannyn Porter and Ursula Handleigh

events.title: Ewww...that Science is Gross!
events.description: Discover the science behind things that make you go...ewww! Make "snot" and sneeze it while exploring viscosity, identify various animals from their scat (i.e. poop) and learn about digestion while producing a burp in a bag. Join us on a gross but fun-filled science adventure!

The program concludes with a Comic Con showcase, where their work is shared with the community. For ages 9-12. Spaces are limited.  Registration required.

events.title: Indigenous Film Screening - Nechako: It Will Be a Big River Again
events.description: Nechako is a crucial documentary from Lyana Patrick that follows two Indigenous Nations fighting for our collective future. When the Kenney Dam was built in the 1950s, it diverted 70 percent of the Nechako River into an artificial reservoir, severely impacting the lives of local Stellat’en and Saik’uz Nations. What followed were decades of resistance, including legal actions against the Canadian federal and provincial governments and Rio Tinto Alcan, a subsidiary of a global mining conglomerate.

events.title: Music Copyright and Ethics in the AI Era
events.description: When you use AI to create music, who actually owns it? Can a melody generated by a machine infringe on someone else's copyright? In this session, you'll get a plain-language breakdown of how music copyright works, what the rules are around sampling, and how these long-standing principles apply to AI-generated music. This is a drop-in program. Please note that there will be no chess club on Saturday May 16 for our Big Book Sale.

events.title: Introduction to 3D Printing
events.description: Learn how 3D printers work and how you can print at the Toronto Public Library’s Digital Innovation Hubs. This class includes hands-on instruction with Prusa Slicer, the software used to prepare your 3D object file for printing on a Prusa MK3S+ 3D printer. Note: Customers are no longer required to attend a mandatory 3D printing class prior to using a 3D printer at the Toronto Public Library’s Digital Innovation Hubs. events.title: Hands-On Dinosaur Fossils & Ancient Life
events.description: Join us for an opportunity to handle, examine up close and discuss nine real and replica fossils in this fascinating hands-on workshop. Participants will be entertained and educated, not just about the fossils themselves, but the paleontological aspects that underpin them. Concepts include: evolution, fossilization, ‘The Tree of Life’ (phylogeny) and — of course — dinosaurs! For children age 9+. events.title: Photo Editing with Photoshop
events.description: Adobe Photoshop is the world’s best imaging and graphic design software and is at the core of just about every creative project, from photo editing and compositing to digital painting, animation, and graphic design. events.title: Botanical Photography / Sunprints
events.description: An alternative eco-friendly photography process that uses botanicals as both its subject matter and development process. I will teach participants how to make their own simple chemical-free photography developer and discuss alternative negatives. Participants will be encouraged to use bring plants from home, leaves found on site, etc to use for the process. This safe chemistry-free activity is adaptable for a broad group of ages Led by Ursula Handleigh

events.title: The Hidden Rivers
events.description: Participants will learn about the hidden rivers in Toronto and explore how our changing cityscape impacts on our waterways and ecosystems. Looking at archival images and current references, participants will be guided to bring their own stories into our city’s images using material such as watercolours, crayons, playdough, text, etc onto colouring pages. Led by Shannyn Porter and Ursula Handleigh

events.title: Youth Hub: Board Game Café
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Tuesday to play board games with your friends! Ask us for our list of board games, and check our monthly calendar for Tournaments with prizes! All materials provided.  This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Youth Hub: Study Hall Wednesdays
events.description: On Wednesdays, the Eatonville Youth Hub becomes a quiet space for teens to study, work on projects, and request homework help and resume help from staff. Bring your homework and enjoy a snack in a space that is just for teens. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find

events.title: Knitting and Crochet Circle
events.description: Spend an afternoon at our weekly drop-in knitting and crochet circle! Bring your work in progress and enjoy visiting with other knitters and crocheters. A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.

events.title: Youth Hub: Crafty Thursdays
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Thursday for a series of crafting activities like painting, clay sculpting, sewing and embroidery, resin art, jewelry making, perler beads, block printing, and more! Check tpl.ca or our monthly calendar for the activity of the week. All materials provided. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily.

events.title: Soundscape
events.description: Next we will be inspired by the library’s architecture and happenings as we are guided by musician Aniqa Qadir to create a soundscape. Working in groups, visitors will be prompted to absorb the sounds of the building and led through the process of creating an audio journey. This playful session welcomes participants to break the whisper norm of a library and interrupt the space with their observations. Led by Aniqa Qadir and Ursula Handleigh

events.title: Taxation and Government Benefits
events.description: Presented by Riverdale Immigrant Women’s Centre (RIWC) , this workshop will provide guidance on tax filing, understanding available benefits and credits and its eligibility requirements, and how to access applicable government financial supports. Registration encouraged . Drop-ins are welcome until capacity is reached.

events.title: Book Club
events.description: Come and join us on the third Thursday of every other month to discuss a variety of books. Everyone is welcome! Drop in, no registration required. This year we are reading:  February 19 -  The Strangers by Katherena Vermette April 16 -  Big Little Lies by Liane Moriarty June 18 -  On the Ravine by Vincent Lam September 17 -  Tomorrow, and Tomorrow, and Tomorrow by Gabrielle Zevin November 19 -  Small Things Like These by

events.title: Youth Hub: Mindful Mondays
events.description: Every Monday, enjoy a series of stress-relieving mindfulness activities, crafts, and games to help ease anxiety and give you a moment of Zen. Enjoy mindful colouring sheets, rainbow scratch art, mindfulness card decks, gratitude journals, and more. All materials provided.  This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by

events.title: NatureTO: Trees Around the Branch Tour
events.description: The Brookbanks branch and its neighbourhood is home to a variety of trees. Join the City of Toronto Urban Forestry team to explore the natural world on a local neighbourhood tour. Learn how to recognize different species and fun facts about their cultural and ecological importance. Free program.  Registration will open up one month before the program date. Children under 12 must be accompanied by a parent/caregiver. events.title: Youth Hub: Sushi Making with Chef Sang Kim
events.description: Join us for a delicious Sushi Making workshop presented by TV host and celebrity chef, Sang Kim, who will teach you the basics of sushi-making. Participants will make sushi rolls using ingredients prepared by the Chef and eat their own creations. A brief history of sushi, its origins and eating etiquette will also be shared. events.title: Youth Hub: Finding a Job with ACCES Employment
events.description: Workplace Readiness & Soft Skill This session focuses on essential workplace skills such as communication, teamwork, time management, and professionalism. It is designed to help youth understand workplace expectations and succeed in their first roles. For teen sessions, bringing a resume is optional. Drop-in, no registration required. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available.&nbs

events.title: Common Misconceptions About Investing
events.description: Presented by Riverdale Immigrant Women’s Centre (RIWC) , this workshop helps to address myths about investing and introduces simple, beginner-friendly investment concepts only. Registration encouraged. Drop-ins are welcome until capacity is reached.
